title: Manifest module: AirlockManifest struct, canonical JSON emission
description:
## Goal
Implement the primary proof object — the airlock manifest. This is the most important output artifact. Every verification produces a manifest, even for BOUNDARY_FAILED.

## Deliverables

### src/manifest/mod.rs — Manifest types and emission

#### AirlockManifest struct
All fields from the plan's Artifact Model section:
- `manifest_version: String` — "airlock.v0"
- `boundary_mode: BoundaryMode` — from src/types.rs
- `policy_id: String`
- `policy_hash: String` — blake3:{hex}
- `claim_levels_evaluated: Vec<ClaimLevel>`
- `achieved_claim: ClaimLevel` — the verdict, from src/types.rs
- `upstream_artifact_inventory: Vec<UpstreamArtifact>` — from src/types.rs
- `system_prompt_hash: String` — blake3:{hex}, first-class field per invariant #2
- `prompt_payload_hash: String` — blake3:{hex}, deterministic core hash
- `prompt_provenance_hash: String` — blake3:{hex}
- `prompt_provenance_ref: String` — co-located path or pack member ID
- `request_payload_hash: String` — blake3:{hex}, full transport artifact hash
- `replay_ref: String` — co-located path for request payload
- `model_id: String`
- `adapter: String`
- `raw_document_present: bool`
- `filing_derived_text_present: bool`
- `findings: Vec<Finding>` — from src/types.rs
- `blocked_reasons: Vec<BlockedReason>` — from src/types.rs
- `provenance_summary: ProvenanceSummary` — aggregate fragment counts by boundary class
- `timestamp: String` — ISO 8601

#### ProvenanceSummary struct
```rust
pub struct ProvenanceSummary {
    pub total_fragments: usize,
    pub by_boundary_class: BTreeMap<String, usize>,
}
```
Built from prompt_provenance during manifest construction. The explain command reads this to render the fragment breakdown (e.g. "25 TELEMETRY, 2 DERIVED_TEXT") without needing the full provenance file.

Note: BoundaryMode, ClaimLevel, Finding, BlockedReason, UpstreamArtifact are defined in src/types.rs (al-1bo scaffold) and imported here. This avoids circular deps with scanner/assembler.

#### Emission
- `manifest.to_canonical_json() -> String` — uses sort_value() for deterministic output
- `manifest.hash() -> String` — blake3 of canonical JSON bytes
- `manifest.write_to(path: &Path) -> Result<(), std::io::Error>` — atomic write

#### Builder pattern
- `ManifestBuilder` for constructing manifests from scanner results + hash references
- Validates all required fields are present before build()
- Accepts PromptProvenance and computes ProvenanceSummary automatically

## Acceptance Criteria
- AirlockManifest contains every field from the plan's artifact model PLUS provenance_summary
- ProvenanceSummary.total_fragments equals the number of ProvenanceRecords
- ProvenanceSummary.by_boundary_class counts match actual provenance data
- Canonical JSON output has recursively sorted keys at every nesting level
- Same manifest struct produces identical JSON bytes across multiple serializations (determinism test)
- Manifest hash is deterministic (hash of canonical JSON)
- ManifestBuilder validates required fields (missing field → descriptive error)
- timestamp format is ISO 8601
- All hash fields match blake3:{hex} format
- Unit tests: construct manifest with all claim levels, verify JSON determinism, verify builder validation, verify provenance_summary computation

title: Project scaffold: Cargo.toml, main.rs, lib.rs, core utilities, build profile
description:
## Goal
Bootstrap the airlock Rust project with the full spine-tool scaffold. This is the foundation every other bead depends on.

## Deliverables

### Cargo.toml
- Package name: `airlock`
- Edition 2021, rust-version = "1.80"
- Pinned dependencies matching spine convention:
  - clap = { version = "=4.5.60", features = ["derive"] }
  - serde = { version = "=1.0.225", features = ["derive"] }
  - serde_json = "=1.0.143"
  - serde_yaml = "=0.9.34"
  - blake3 = "=1.8.2"
  - sha2 = "=0.10"
  - chrono = "=0.4.41"
  - regex = "=1.11"
- Dev dependencies:
  - assert_cmd = "2"
  - predicates = "3"
  - tempfile = "3"
  - jsonschema = "0.18" (or latest)
- Release profile: opt-level = "z", lto = true, codegen-units = 1, panic = "abort", strip = true

### src/main.rs
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`
- Thin entrypoint: `fn main() -> ExitCode { ExitCode::from(airlock::run()) }`

### src/lib.rs
- `pub fn run() -> u8` — minimal stub that returns 0. Real dispatch added by al-jwl.

### src/types.rs — Shared domain types
These are used across policy, manifest, scanner, assembler, and provenance modules. Defined here to avoid circular dependencies.

```rust
// Boundary modes
pub enum BoundaryMode { Annotated, TelemetryOnly }
// Serializes as "ANNOTATED" / "TELEMETRY_ONLY"

// Claim levels (strict ordering)
pub enum ClaimLevel { BoundaryFailed, RawDocumentAbsent, StrictTelemetryOnly }
// Implements Ord: BoundaryFailed < RawDocumentAbsent < StrictTelemetryOnly
// Serializes as SCREAMING_SNAKE

// Finding — offending item detected during scanning
pub struct Finding {
    pub key_path: String,
    pub sample_value: Option<String>,  // truncated to 100 chars
    pub artifact_class: String,
    pub matched_rule: String,
}

// BlockedReason — why a stronger claim was not earned
pub struct BlockedReason {
    pub claim_attempted: ClaimLevel,
    pub reason: String,
    pub offending_paths: Vec<String>,
}

// UpstreamArtifact — an input that influenced the boundary crossing
pub struct UpstreamArtifact {
    pub path: String,
    pub content_hash: String,
    pub artifact_class: String,
}
```

All types derive Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, Clone, PartialEq.

### src/hash.rs — Hashing utilities
- `blake3_hash(data: &[u8]) -> String` — returns `blake3:{hex}` spine format
- `sha256_hash(data: &[u8]) -> String` — returns `sha256:{hex}` fallback format
- `hash_file(path: &Path) -> Result<String, std::io::Error>` — read file bytes, return blake3 hash

### src/output.rs — Canonical JSON utilities
- `sort_value(v: serde_json::Value) -> Value` — recursive canonical JSON key sorting at all nesting levels
- `canonical_json(v: &Value) -> String` — sort then serialize
- `canonical_json_bytes(v: &Value) -> Vec<u8>` — for hashing

### src/cli/mod.rs + src/cli/exit.rs — CLI stub
- `pub const VERIFY_PASS: u8 = 0;`
- `pub const VERIFY_PARTIAL: u8 = 1;`
- `pub const REFUSAL: u8 = 2;`
- Empty mod.rs re-exporting exit codes

### Other files
- LICENSE (MIT)
- Empty module stubs: src/policy/mod.rs, src/assembler/mod.rs, src/scanner/mod.rs, src/manifest/mod.rs, src/adapter/mod.rs, src/refusal/mod.rs, src/witness/mod.rs
- schemas/ directory (empty)
- fixtures/ directory (empty)
- tests/ directory (empty)
- .gitignore (target/, *.swp, etc.)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo build` succeeds
- `cargo test` passes (even if only default test)
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean
- `cargo fmt --check` clean
- `sort_value` correctly sorts nested keys (unit test)
- `blake3_hash` returns `blake3:{hex}` format (unit test)
- `canonical_json` is deterministic across key insertion order (unit test)
- `ClaimLevel` ordering: BoundaryFailed < RawDocumentAbsent < StrictTelemetryOnly (unit test)
- `BoundaryMode` serde round-trip: ANNOTATED, TELEMETRY_ONLY (unit test)
- `Finding`, `BlockedReason`, `UpstreamArtifact` serde round-trip (unit test)
- main.rs stays under 10 lines
- lib.rs run() returns 0

title: Verify command: end-to-end verification with manifest emission and --require-claim
description:
## Goal
Wire the verify subcommand end-to-end: load policy + prompt + provenance + request, run the scanner, emit airlock_manifest.json, support --require-claim gating, record witness.

## Deliverables

### Verify command handler

#### Flow
1. Load and validate policy file → RefusalEnvelope on failure
2. Read and parse prompt_payload.json → RefusalEnvelope on failure
3. Read and parse prompt_provenance.json → RefusalEnvelope on failure
4. Read and parse request.json → RefusalEnvelope on failure
5. Hash all input artifacts (blake3)
6. Determine boundary_mode from provenance metadata (or from flag if provided)
7. Call scanner::verify(policy, prompt, provenance, request, boundary_mode)
8. Build AirlockManifest with:
   - All hash references (policy, prompt, provenance, request, system prompt)
   - Upstream artifact inventory from provenance
   - Scanner results (findings, blocked_reasons, achieved_claim)
   - Boundary mode, model_id, adapter from provenance/request metadata
   - replay_ref and prompt_provenance_ref paths
   - Timestamp (ISO 8601)
9. Write airlock_manifest.json to --out path (canonical JSON)
10. Apply --require-claim gating:
    - If achieved_claim >= require_claim → exit 0 (VERIFY_PASS)
    - If achieved_claim < require_claim → exit 1 (VERIFY_PARTIAL)
    - Manifest is ALWAYS written regardless of gating result
11. Record witness (unless --no-witness):
    - outcome: VERIFIED or VERIFY_PARTIAL
    - inputs: all file paths+hashes
12. Print manifest JSON to stdout

#### Exit code logic (critical)
- BOUNDARY_FAILED verdict → exit 0 (tool ran correctly, produced honest manifest)
- BOUNDARY_FAILED + --require-claim RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT → exit 1 (claim below threshold)
- RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT + --require-claim RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT → exit 0
- RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT + --require-claim STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY → exit 1
- STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY + any --require-claim → exit 0

#### Error paths
- Any input file missing → RefusalEnvelope, exit 2
- Invalid JSON in any input → RefusalEnvelope, exit 2
- Policy parse failure → RefusalEnvelope, exit 2

## Acceptance Criteria
- End-to-end test: verify with clean inputs → manifest with correct claim
- Manifest is always emitted, even for BOUNDARY_FAILED
- --require-claim gates exit code correctly (all 5 combinations above)
- Manifest contains all fields from artifact model spec
- Manifest canonical JSON is deterministic
- Missing files → structured refusal
- Witness records VERIFIED or VERIFY_PARTIAL correctly
- --no-witness → no witness record
- Exit code 2 only for refusals, never for domain outcomes

title: OpenAI adapter: thin transport mapping from prompt_payload to request envelope
description:
## Goal
Implement the first thin transport adapter: OpenAI Chat Completions API format. The adapter maps (prompt_payload, transport_config) → request_payload. It MUST NOT inject semantic content.

## Deliverables

### src/adapter/mod.rs

#### TransportConfig struct
- `model_id: String` — e.g. "gpt-4", "o3"
- `temperature: Option<f64>`
- `max_tokens: Option<u32>`
- `response_format: Option<Value>` — vendor-specific response format
- `additional_params: Option<Map<String, Value>>` — pass-through for vendor-specific fields

#### Adapter trait (if multiple adapters later)
```rust
pub trait Adapter {
    fn name(&self) -> &str;
    fn wrap(&self, prompt_payload: &Value, config: &TransportConfig) -> Result<Value, RefusalEnvelope>;
}
```

#### OpenAI Chat Completions adapter
- Takes prompt_payload (system message + user message content)
- Wraps into OpenAI Chat Completions format:
```json
{
  "model": "gpt-4",
  "messages": [
    {"role": "system", "content": "..."},
    {"role": "user", "content": "..."}
  ],
  "temperature": 0.0,
  "max_tokens": 4096,
  "response_format": {...}
}
```
- Adapter name: "openai_chat_completions"

#### Critical constraint: no semantic injection
- Adapters may reshape envelopes
- Adapters may NOT inject semantic content that lacks provenance coverage
- The adapter adds only transport-layer fields: model, temperature, max_tokens, response_format
- The content of messages comes entirely from prompt_payload

#### Request payload hashing
- The adapter output is the request_payload
- Caller hashes it with blake3 for the manifest's request_payload_hash

## Acceptance Criteria
- Adapter wraps prompt_payload into valid OpenAI Chat Completions format
- Adapter adds only transport fields, no semantic content
- Round-trip: unwrap the adapter output, content matches original prompt_payload
- Missing model_id → RefusalEnvelope
- adapter.name() returns "openai_chat_completions"
- Output JSON keys are canonical (sorted)
- Unit tests: basic wrap, verify no extra content beyond transport fields, missing config

title: Witness module: spine ledger protocol, append/query/last/count
description:
## Goal
Implement the spine witness ledger protocol. Airlock records a witness entry for eligible invocations (assemble, verify) and exposes the standard query surface (query, last, count).

## Deliverables

### src/witness/mod.rs

#### Witness record format (witness.v0)
```json
{
  "witness_version": "witness.v0",
  "tool": "airlock",
  "command": "verify",
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "outcome": "VERIFIED",
  "exit_code": 0,
  "inputs": [
    {"path": "policy.yaml", "hash": "blake3:abc..."},
    {"path": "prompt.json", "hash": "blake3:def..."}
  ],
  "params": {"boundary_mode": "ANNOTATED", "require_claim": null},
  "stdout_hash": "blake3:ghi...",
  "stdout_bytes": 4096
}
```

#### Ledger path resolution
1. `EPISTEMIC_WITNESS` env var (if set)
2. `~/.epistemic/witness.jsonl` (default)
3. Create parent directories if needed

#### Recording policy (v0)
- Record for: `assemble`, `verify`
- Do NOT record for: `witness` subcommands, `--describe`, `--schema`, `--version`
- `explain` is non-recording in v0

#### Outcome mapping
- `assemble` → ASSEMBLED or REFUSAL
- `verify` → VERIFIED, VERIFY_PARTIAL, or REFUSAL

#### Append behavior
- `append_witness(record: WitnessRecord) -> Result<(), WitnessError>`
- Append one JSONL line to ledger file
- **CRITICAL**: append failure MUST NOT change domain exit semantics
  - If append fails, log to stderr, but return the original domain exit code
  - Never let a witness I/O error override a successful domain result

#### --no-witness support
- When `--no-witness` flag is present, skip append entirely
- Clean, early opt-out path

#### Query surface
- `query(filters: WitnessFilters) -> Vec<WitnessRecord>`
- `last() -> Option<WitnessRecord>`
- `count(filters: WitnessFilters) -> usize`

#### WitnessFilters
- `tool: Option<String>` (default: "airlock")
- `since: Option<DateTime>`
- `until: Option<DateTime>`
- `outcome: Option<String>`
- `input_hash: Option<String>` (substring match)
- `limit: Option<usize>`

#### Query exit semantics
- exit 0: successful query with matches
- exit 1: successful query with no matches
- exit 2: ledger error or refusal

## Acceptance Criteria
- Append writes valid JSONL line to ledger
- Append failure does not change domain exit code (critical test)
- --no-witness skips append entirely
- Ledger path resolves from env, then default
- Query filters work: by outcome, by date range, by input hash substring
- last() returns most recent record
- count() returns correct count with filters
- Query on empty/missing ledger returns 0 matches (exit 1), not error
- Unit tests: append, append failure isolation, query filters, last, count, empty ledger

title: Policy module: AirlockPolicy types, YAML parsing, policy hashing
description:
## Goal
Implement the policy engine that defines what is allowed and forbidden at the model boundary. This is the rule substrate that the assembler and scanner operate against.

## Deliverables

### src/policy/types.rs — Policy types
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct AirlockPolicy {
    pub policy_id: String,
    pub version: String,
    pub allowed_keys: Vec<AllowRule>,
    pub forbidden_keys: Vec<String>,
    pub forbidden_patterns: Vec<ForbiddenPattern>,
    pub derived_text_paths: Vec<String>,
    pub claim_levels: Vec<String>,
}
```

- `AllowRule` struct: key path, optional description, optional boundary class override
- `DenyRule` / `ForbiddenPattern` struct: pattern (regex string), description, artifact class
- All types derive Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, Clone

### src/policy/mod.rs — Parsing and hashing
- `load_policy(path: &Path) -> Result<AirlockPolicy, RefusalEnvelope>` — parse YAML, validate required fields
- `hash_policy(path: &Path) -> String` — read raw bytes, compute `blake3:{hex}` hash of the file content
- Policy validation: policy_id non-empty, version non-empty, at least one allow rule, claim_levels non-empty and must be valid enum values
- On parse failure, return structured RefusalEnvelope with code E_BAD_POLICY

### Example policy file: rules/tournament_baseline.yaml
A complete example policy matching the BDC tournament reference:
- Allow: family_id, current_strategy, allowed_toggles, max_proposals, benchmark_summary, unresolved_failure_fields, recent_lineage_rows, heuristic_reference, mutator_context (with sub-field rules)
- Forbid keys: html_path, section_html, pdf_path, page_images
- Forbid patterns: raw HTML markup (<[a-z][^>]*>), SEC archive URLs (sec.gov/Archives)
- Derived text paths: mutator_context.challenge_observation, mutator_context.challenge_profile.header_surfaces, mutator_context.challenge_profile.layout_notes
- Claim levels: BOUNDARY_FAILED, RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT, STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY

## Acceptance Criteria
- Round-trip test: serialize → deserialize → serialize produces identical YAML
- Policy hash is deterministic (same file = same hash every time)
- Bad YAML returns RefusalEnvelope, not panic
- Missing required fields return clear refusal
- Forbidden patterns compile as valid regex (test with known good and bad patterns)
- Example policy file validates successfully
- Unit tests cover: valid policy, missing policy_id, invalid regex pattern, empty allowed_keys

title: Assemble command: end-to-end prompt assembly with file I/O and witness
description:
## Goal
Wire the assemble subcommand end-to-end: load policy, read input files, run the assembler, emit prompt_payload.json + prompt_provenance.json, record witness.

## Deliverables

### Assemble command handler (in src/lib.rs or src/cli/ dispatch)

#### Flow
1. Load and validate policy file → RefusalEnvelope on failure
2. Read and hash each --input file → RefusalEnvelope on missing/invalid
3. Read system prompt file if --system-prompt provided, else use empty/default
4. Parse --boundary-mode (ANNOTATED default)
5. Call assembler::assemble(policy, inputs, system_prompt, boundary_mode)
6. Write prompt_payload.json to --out path (canonical JSON)
7. Write prompt_provenance.json to --provenance-out path (canonical JSON)
8. Record witness (unless --no-witness):
   - outcome: ASSEMBLED
   - inputs: policy path+hash, each input path+hash
   - stdout_hash: hash of prompt_payload.json bytes
   - stdout_bytes: byte length of prompt_payload.json
9. Print confirmation to stdout (the prompt_payload.json content)
10. Exit 0

#### Error paths
- Policy load failure → RefusalEnvelope (E_BAD_POLICY), exit 2
- Input file missing → RefusalEnvelope (E_MISSING_FILE), exit 2
- Input file invalid JSON → RefusalEnvelope (E_BAD_INPUT), exit 2
- Output path not writable → RefusalEnvelope (E_BAD_INPUT), exit 2
- Witness append failure → stderr warning, but still exit 0

## Acceptance Criteria
- End-to-end test: assemble with valid policy + inputs → prompt_payload.json + prompt_provenance.json exist and are valid JSON
- Determinism test: run twice, both output files are byte-identical
- Multiple --input files all appear in upstream_artifact_inventory of provenance
- Missing policy → structured refusal with E_BAD_POLICY
- Missing input → structured refusal with E_MISSING_FILE
- --no-witness → no witness record appended
- --boundary-mode TELEMETRY_ONLY strips derived text fields from output
- Exit code is always 0 on success, 2 on refusal

title: Explain command: human-readable manifest rendering
description:
## Goal
Implement `airlock explain --manifest <PATH>` — renders a manifest into a concise, human-readable verdict summary. Stakeholders should understand the proof without reading raw JSON.

## Deliverables

### Explain command handler

#### Flow
1. Load and parse airlock_manifest.json → RefusalEnvelope on failure
2. Render human-readable output to stdout

#### Output format (from README example)
```text
AIRLOCK

Claim achieved: RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT
Boundary mode: ANNOTATED

Raw document artifacts: absent
Derived filing text: present

Why strict telemetry-only was not earned:
  - mutator_context.challenge_observation
  - mutator_context.challenge_profile.layout_notes[0]

Prompt provenance:
  - 27 prompt fragments emitted
  - 25 TELEMETRY
  - 2 DERIVED_TEXT

Policy: tournament_baseline (blake3:abc123...)
Manifest hash: blake3:def456...
```

#### Rendering rules
- Always show: claim achieved, boundary mode
- Always show: raw_document_present, filing_derived_text_present (as human words)
- If BOUNDARY_FAILED: show "Boundary violation detected:" followed by findings
- If not STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY: show "Why strict telemetry-only was not earned:" with blocked_reasons
- Show prompt provenance summary: total fragments, count by boundary_class
- Show policy_id and policy_hash
- Show manifest hash
- Do NOT show timestamps, full upstream inventory, or other verbose fields (those are in the JSON)
- Non-recording in v0 (no witness append for explain)

#### Exit code
- exit 0: manifest loaded and rendered
- exit 2: manifest file missing or invalid (RefusalEnvelope)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Explain renders each claim level correctly (BOUNDARY_FAILED, RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT, STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY)
- Explain shows blocked reasons when claim < STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY
- Explain shows findings when BOUNDARY_FAILED
- Missing manifest → structured refusal
- Invalid manifest JSON → structured refusal
- Output is plain text on stdout, not JSON
- No witness recording for explain
- Unit tests: explain for each claim level, explain for missing manifest

title: Introspection + operator.json + output module: --describe, --schema, --version
description:
## Goal
Implement the spine-standard introspection flags and the operator.json metadata surface. These are used by pipeline orchestrators to discover tool capabilities.

## Deliverables

### operator.json (project root, embedded via include_str!)
Full operator metadata per plan spec:
```json
{
  "name": "airlock",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Prove what crossed the model boundary",
  "pipeline_position": {
    "upstream": ["vacuum", "hash", "fingerprint", "benchmark", "assess"],
    "downstream": ["lock", "pack", "catalog"]
  },
  "subcommands": {
    "assemble": {
      "description": "Deterministically assemble a prompt payload from approved telemetry",
      "usage": "airlock assemble --policy <POLICY> --input <FILE>... --out <PROMPT> --provenance-out <PROVENANCE>"
    },
    "verify": {
      "description": "Verify prompt and request payloads against policy, emit manifest",
      "usage": "airlock verify --policy <POLICY> --prompt <PROMPT> --provenance <PROVENANCE> --request <REQUEST> --out <MANIFEST>"
    },
    "explain": {
      "description": "Render human-readable manifest explanation",
      "usage": "airlock explain --manifest <MANIFEST>"
    },
    "witness": {
      "description": "Query the witness ledger",
      "usage": "airlock witness <query|last|count> [filters] [--json]"
    }
  },
  "common_flags": ["--describe", "--schema", "--version", "--no-witness"],
  "exit_semantics": {
    "0": "VERIFY_PASS — verification completed and caller requirement was met",
    "1": "VERIFY_PARTIAL — achieved claim fell below caller requirement or boundary-mode ceiling",
    "2": "REFUSAL — bad input, missing policy, or unrecoverable error"
  },
  "refusal_codes": ["E_BAD_POLICY", "E_BAD_INPUT", "E_BAD_PROMPT", "E_BAD_REQUEST", "E_BAD_PROVENANCE", "E_BAD_MANIFEST", "E_MISSING_FILE", "E_WITNESS_ERROR", "E_INTERNAL"],
  "witness": {
    "tool_name": "airlock",
    "recording_commands": ["assemble", "verify"],
    "non_recording_commands": ["explain", "witness"],
    "outcomes": ["ASSEMBLED", "VERIFIED", "VERIFY_PARTIAL", "REFUSAL"]
  },
  "output_mode": "json",
  "output_schema": "airlock.v0.schema.json"
}
```

### schemas/airlock.v0.schema.json
The output JSON schema, embedded via include_str! and emitted by --schema.
Defines the manifest output format as JSON Schema.

### src/output/mod.rs additions
- `emit_describe()` — print operator.json to stdout, exit 0
- `emit_schema()` — print output schema to stdout, exit 0  
- `emit_version()` — print "airlock {version}" to stdout, exit 0
- Version string sourced from Cargo.toml via env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")
- `format_jsonl_line(v: &Value) -> String` — single-line canonical JSON for JSONL output

### Introspection precedence (in lib.rs dispatch)
1. --describe → emit_describe() → exit 0
2. --schema → emit_schema() → exit 0
3. --version → emit_version() → exit 0
4. Subcommand → dispatch

## Acceptance Criteria
- `airlock --describe` emits valid JSON matching operator.json schema
- `airlock --schema` emits valid JSON Schema
- `airlock --version` emits "airlock X.Y.Z" format
- Precedence: --describe wins over --schema, --schema wins over --version, all win over subcommands
- operator.json includes all subcommands, exit semantics, refusal codes, witness capabilities
- include_str! embeds operator.json and schema at compile time
- Unit tests: verify each introspection flag output, verify precedence ordering

title: Scanner/verifier: boundary scanning, claim derivation, findings generation
description:
## Goal
Implement the boundary scanner that verifies a prompt payload + request payload against policy and derives the claim level verdict. This is the judgment engine — it decides BOUNDARY_FAILED vs RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT vs STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY.

## Deliverables

### src/scanner/mod.rs

#### Core function
```rust
pub fn verify(
    policy: &AirlockPolicy,
    prompt_payload: &Value,
    prompt_provenance: &PromptProvenance,
    request_payload: &Value,
    boundary_mode: BoundaryMode,
) -> VerifyResult
```

#### VerifyResult struct
- `achieved_claim: ClaimLevel`
- `raw_document_present: bool`
- `filing_derived_text_present: bool`
- `findings: Vec<Finding>`
- `blocked_reasons: Vec<BlockedReason>`

#### Scanning phases (ordered)

**Phase 1: Forbidden key scan**
- Walk all key paths in prompt_payload and request_payload
- Check against policy.forbidden_keys
- If any match → add Finding, set raw_document_present = true

**Phase 2: Forbidden pattern scan**
- For each value in prompt_payload and request_payload (recursive string extraction)
- Check against policy.forbidden_patterns (compiled regex)
- Match raw HTML markup, SEC archive URLs, PDF paths, etc.
- If any match → add Finding with matched_rule, sample_value (truncated), set raw_document_present = true

**Phase 3: Derived text field scan**
- Walk prompt_payload for keys matching policy.derived_text_paths
- If boundary_mode == TELEMETRY_ONLY and derived text fields are present → this is an anomaly (assembler bug)
- If boundary_mode == ANNOTATED and derived text fields present → expected, record in findings.derived_text_field_hits
- Set filing_derived_text_present accordingly

**Phase 4: Claim derivation**
- If Phase 1 or Phase 2 found forbidden material → BOUNDARY_FAILED
- If filing_derived_text_present → RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT (ceiling for ANNOTATED mode)
- If no forbidden material AND no derived text → STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY
- Boundary mode ceiling: ANNOTATED mode caps at RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT even if no derived text found (honest ceiling)

**Phase 5: Blocked reasons**
- For each claim level above achieved_claim, explain why it wasn't earned
- Include offending key paths from findings

#### Provenance cross-check
- Verify that every field in prompt_payload has a corresponding ProvenanceRecord
- If provenance gaps exist, add a finding (does not necessarily degrade claim, but records the gap)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clean payload + TELEMETRY_ONLY → STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY
- Clean payload + ANNOTATED (no derived text) → RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT (mode ceiling)
- Payload with derived text + ANNOTATED → RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT with derived_text_field_hits
- Payload with forbidden key → BOUNDARY_FAILED with findings listing the key
- Payload with forbidden pattern (HTML) → BOUNDARY_FAILED with findings including sample
- Blocked reasons correctly explain why each higher claim failed
- Provenance cross-check catches missing provenance records
- Sample values in findings are truncated (max 100 chars)
- Unit tests: clean scan, forbidden key, forbidden pattern, derived text, boundary mode ceiling, provenance gap

title: Witness subcommands: query/last/count CLI surface with filters
description:
## Goal
Implement the `airlock witness` subcommand family: query, last, count. These expose the spine-standard ledger query surface.

## Deliverables

### Witness subcommand handlers

#### `airlock witness query`
- Reads witness ledger file
- Applies filters: --tool, --since, --until, --outcome, --input-hash, --limit
- Default output: human-readable table (one line per record)
- --json: output as JSON array
- exit 0 with matches, exit 1 with no matches, exit 2 on ledger error

#### `airlock witness last`
- Returns the most recent witness record from the ledger
- Default: human-readable single record
- --json: output as JSON object
- exit 0 if record found, exit 1 if ledger empty, exit 2 on error

#### `airlock witness count`
- Same filters as query
- Default output: plain integer count
- --json: output as {"count": N}
- exit 0 if count > 0, exit 1 if count == 0, exit 2 on error

#### Human-readable output format for query
```text
WITNESS LEDGER (4 records)

2024-01-15T10:30:00Z  verify   VERIFIED        policy.yaml → manifest.json
2024-01-15T10:29:00Z  assemble ASSEMBLED       strategy_space.json → prompt.json
2024-01-15T09:15:00Z  verify   VERIFY_PARTIAL  policy.yaml → manifest.json
2024-01-15T09:14:00Z  assemble ASSEMBLED       strategy_space.json → prompt.json
```

#### Filter implementation
- --tool: exact match on tool field (default "airlock" for airlock witness queries)
- --since/--until: ISO 8601 datetime comparison on timestamp
- --outcome: exact match on outcome field
- --input-hash: substring match on any input hash in the record
- --limit: cap number of results returned

#### Non-recording
- Witness subcommands NEVER append witness records themselves
- No --no-witness flag needed (it's implicit)

## Acceptance Criteria
- query returns correct results with each filter type
- query with no filters returns all airlock records
- query with --limit caps results
- last returns most recent record
- count returns correct count
- --json produces valid JSON for all three subcommands
- Empty/missing ledger → exit 1 (not error), human message "No witness records found"
- Corrupted ledger line → skip and continue (resilient parsing)
- Non-recording: running witness query does not add to ledger
- Unit tests: query filters, last on empty/populated ledger, count with filters, JSON output format

title: Prompt provenance types: ProvenanceRecord, enums, artifact shape
description:
## Goal
Define the proof-carrying prompt source map types. prompt_provenance.json is the most leveraged concept in airlock — it turns the system from "we scanned the request" into "we can account for why every emitted fragment exists."

## Deliverables

### src/assembler/provenance.rs

#### ProvenanceRecord struct
One record per emitted prompt field or message fragment:
- `prompt_path: String` — locator in the assembled prompt (e.g. "messages[1].content.challenge_profile.page_count")
- `emitted_value_hash: String` — blake3:{hex} of the emitted value
- `source_artifact_hash: String` — blake3:{hex} of the upstream artifact that produced this value
- `source_path: String` — key path within the source artifact
- `transformation_kind: TransformationKind`
- `policy_rule_id: String` — which allow rule admitted this fragment
- `boundary_class: BoundaryClass`
- `admitted_boundary_modes: Vec<BoundaryMode>` — uses BoundaryMode from src/types.rs

#### TransformationKind enum
- COPY — value copied verbatim from source
- NORMALIZE — value normalized (whitespace, casing, etc.)
- AGGREGATE — value aggregated from multiple source fields
- TEMPLATE — value produced by template expansion
- DERIVED_ANNOTATION — filing-derived descriptive text
Serializes as SCREAMING_SNAKE.

#### BoundaryClass enum
- TELEMETRY — structured telemetry data, always allowed
- DERIVED_TEXT — filing-derived prose, only in ANNOTATED mode
- FORBIDDEN — should never cross (indicates a bug if present)
Serializes as SCREAMING_SNAKE.

#### PromptProvenance container
- `version: String` — "airlock.v0"
- `policy_hash: String` — blake3:{hex}
- `prompt_payload_hash: String` — blake3:{hex} of the prompt this provenance describes
- `boundary_mode: BoundaryMode` — from src/types.rs
- `records: Vec<ProvenanceRecord>`
- `to_canonical_json() -> String`
- `hash() -> String` — blake3 of canonical JSON bytes

Note: BoundaryMode imported from src/types.rs (al-1bo scaffold). TransformationKind and BoundaryClass are provenance-specific and defined here.

## Acceptance Criteria
- TransformationKind serializes as COPY, NORMALIZE, AGGREGATE, TEMPLATE, DERIVED_ANNOTATION
- BoundaryClass serializes as TELEMETRY, DERIVED_TEXT, FORBIDDEN
- ProvenanceRecord round-trips through serde
- PromptProvenance canonical JSON is deterministic
- PromptProvenance hash is deterministic
- admitted_boundary_modes correctly uses BoundaryMode from src/types.rs
- Unit tests: construct provenance with mixed boundary classes, verify serialization, verify hash stability

title: Refusal module: RefusalEnvelope, RefusalCode, structured error output
description:
## Goal
Implement the structured error handling system. Airlock uses custom RefusalEnvelope structs, NOT anyhow or thiserror. Every error is a structured JSON object on stdout with exit code 2.

## Deliverables

### src/refusal/mod.rs

#### RefusalCode enum
```rust
pub enum RefusalCode {
    EBadPolicy,        // "E_BAD_POLICY" — policy file failed schema or parse
    EBadInput,         // "E_BAD_INPUT" — input file missing, unreadable, or invalid
    EBadPrompt,        // "E_BAD_PROMPT" — prompt payload invalid
    EBadRequest,       // "E_BAD_REQUEST" — request payload invalid
    EBadProvenance,    // "E_BAD_PROVENANCE" — provenance file invalid
    EBadManifest,      // "E_BAD_MANIFEST" — manifest file invalid (for explain)
    EMissingFile,      // "E_MISSING_FILE" — required file path does not exist
    EWitnessError,     // "E_WITNESS_ERROR" — witness ledger error (query only)
    EInternal,         // "E_INTERNAL" — unexpected internal error
}
```
- Serializes as screaming snake with E_ prefix

#### RefusalEnvelope struct
```json
{
  "version": "airlock.v0",
  "outcome": "REFUSAL",
  "refusal": {
    "code": "E_BAD_POLICY",
    "message": "policy file failed schema validation",
    "detail": { "path": "policy.yaml", "error": "..." },
    "next_command": "airlock --schema"
  }
}
```

- `version: String` — always "airlock.v0"
- `outcome: String` — always "REFUSAL"
- `refusal.code: RefusalCode`
- `refusal.message: String` — human-readable
- `refusal.detail: serde_json::Value` — arbitrary structured detail
- `refusal.next_command: Option<String>` — suggested recovery command

#### Methods
- `RefusalEnvelope::new(code, message, detail, next_command) -> Self`
- `RefusalEnvelope::to_canonical_json(&self) -> String` — canonical sorted JSON
- `RefusalEnvelope::write_to(&self, w: &mut impl Write) -> u8` — write JSON to writer, return exit code 2. This is the testable emit path — callers pass stdout in production and a Vec<u8> in tests.
- Convenience constructors: `RefusalEnvelope::bad_policy(msg, detail)`, `RefusalEnvelope::missing_file(path)`, `RefusalEnvelope::bad_input(msg, detail)`, etc.
- `RefusalEnvelope::exit_code() -> u8` — always returns 2

IMPORTANT: Do NOT call `std::process::exit()` inside refusal methods. The caller (lib.rs run()) uses the returned exit code. This keeps the entire codebase testable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- RefusalEnvelope serializes to canonical sorted JSON
- write_to() writes to provided writer and returns exit code 2
- write_to() is testable with Vec<u8> as writer
- Every RefusalCode round-trips through serde correctly
- RefusalCode serializes as E_BAD_POLICY, E_MISSING_FILE, etc. (screaming snake with E_ prefix)
- next_command is optional (Some or None)
- detail accepts arbitrary JSON (string, object, array, null)
- Convenience constructors produce correct code + default next_command
- Unit tests: construct each refusal code, verify JSON shape, verify code serialization, test write_to with buffer

title: CLI shell + introspection: clap structs, dispatch, operator.json, --describe/--schema/--version
description:
## Goal
Implement the clap-based CLI shell, dispatch routing, and spine-standard introspection surface. This bead owns all of: arg parsing, subcommand dispatch, operator.json, output schema, and the --describe/--schema/--version emit functions.

Merged from original al-jwl (CLI shell) and al-35a (introspection) to avoid file conflicts in lib.rs and src/output.rs.

## Deliverables

### src/cli/mod.rs — Clap derive structs

#### Top-level CLI struct
```rust
#[derive(Parser)]
#[command(name = "airlock", about = "Prove what crossed the model boundary")]
pub struct Cli {
    #[command(subcommand)]
    pub command: Option<Commands>,

    #[arg(long)]
    pub describe: bool,

    #[arg(long)]
    pub schema: bool,

    #[arg(long, alias = "version")]
    pub print_version: bool,
}
```

#### Commands enum
```rust
pub enum Commands {
    Assemble(AssembleArgs),
    Verify(VerifyArgs),
    Explain(ExplainArgs),
    Witness(WitnessCommands),
}
```

#### AssembleArgs
- `--policy <PATH>` (required)
- `--input <PATH>...` (required, multiple)
- `--system-prompt <PATH>` (optional, path to system prompt file)
- `--boundary-mode <MODE>` (optional, default ANNOTATED, validates against BoundaryMode enum)
- `--out <PATH>` (required, prompt_payload.json output)
- `--provenance-out <PATH>` (required, prompt_provenance.json output)
- `--no-witness` (flag)

#### VerifyArgs
- `--policy <PATH>` (required)
- `--prompt <PATH>` (required, prompt_payload.json)
- `--provenance <PATH>` (required, prompt_provenance.json)
- `--request <PATH>` (required, request.json)
- `--out <PATH>` (required, airlock_manifest.json output)
- `--require-claim <LEVEL>` (optional, validates against ClaimLevel enum)
- `--no-witness` (flag)

#### ExplainArgs
- `--manifest <PATH>` (required)

#### WitnessCommands (subcommands of witness)
- `Query` with filter flags: --tool, --since, --until, --outcome, --input-hash, --limit, --json
- `Last` with --json flag
- `Count` with same filter flags as Query, plus --json

### src/lib.rs — Dispatch logic

#### Dispatch pattern
This bead wires the top-level dispatch in lib.rs::run(). Command beads (al-2bi, al-1cc, al-2vh, al-3a7) implement handler functions in their own modules under src/commands/:
- `src/commands/assemble.rs` → `pub fn run(args: AssembleArgs) -> u8`
- `src/commands/verify.rs` → `pub fn run(args: VerifyArgs) -> u8`
- `src/commands/explain.rs` → `pub fn run(args: ExplainArgs) -> u8`
- `src/commands/witness.rs` → `pub fn run(cmd: WitnessCommands) -> u8`

lib.rs dispatch calls these handlers. This means command beads create NEW files (src/commands/*.rs) without touching lib.rs — avoiding merge conflicts between beads.

This bead creates the dispatch skeleton with stub handlers that return 0. Each command bead replaces its stub with the real implementation.

Replace the scaffold stub with full dispatch:
1. Parse CLI with clap
2. Introspection flag precedence: --describe > --schema > --version > subcommand
3. If introspection flag → call emit function → return 0
4. If subcommand → dispatch to handler in src/commands/ → return handler exit code
5. If no subcommand and no flag → print help via clap, return 2

### operator.json (project root, embedded via include_str!)
Full operator metadata:
```json
{
  "name": "airlock",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Prove what crossed the model boundary",
  "pipeline_position": {
    "upstream": ["vacuum", "hash", "fingerprint", "benchmark", "assess"],
    "downstream": ["lock", "pack", "catalog"]
  },
  "subcommands": {
    "assemble": { "description": "...", "usage": "..." },
    "verify": { "description": "...", "usage": "..." },
    "explain": { "description": "...", "usage": "..." },
    "witness": { "description": "...", "usage": "..." }
  },
  "common_flags": ["--describe", "--schema", "--version", "--no-witness"],
  "exit_semantics": { "0": "VERIFY_PASS", "1": "VERIFY_PARTIAL", "2": "REFUSAL" },
  "refusal_codes": ["E_BAD_POLICY", "E_BAD_INPUT", "E_BAD_PROMPT", "E_BAD_REQUEST", "E_BAD_PROVENANCE", "E_BAD_MANIFEST", "E_MISSING_FILE", "E_WITNESS_ERROR", "E_INTERNAL"],
  "witness": {
    "tool_name": "airlock",
    "recording_commands": ["assemble", "verify"],
    "non_recording_commands": ["explain", "witness"],
    "outcomes": ["ASSEMBLED", "VERIFIED", "VERIFY_PARTIAL", "REFUSAL"]
  },
  "output_mode": "json",
  "output_schema": "airlock.v0.schema.json"
}
```

### schemas/airlock.v0.schema.json
The output/manifest JSON schema, embedded via include_str! and emitted by --schema. Defines the manifest output format as JSON Schema draft-07 or 2020-12. This is THE manifest schema — other beads reference it for validation.

### src/output.rs additions
Extend the output module (created by al-1bo with canonical JSON utils) with:
- `emit_describe(w: &mut impl Write)` — write operator.json content
- `emit_schema(w: &mut impl Write)` — write output schema content
- `emit_version(w: &mut impl Write)` — write "airlock {version}" using env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")
- `format_jsonl_line(v: &Value) -> String` — single-line canonical JSON for JSONL

All emit functions take a writer for testability (stdout in production, Vec<u8> in tests).

### src/commands/mod.rs — Module declarations
Declare the command handler modules. Stub implementations that command beads will replace:
```rust
pub mod assemble;
pub mod verify;
pub mod explain;
pub mod witness;
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- `airlock --help` shows all subcommands and common flags
- `airlock assemble --help` shows all assemble flags
- `airlock verify --help` shows all verify flags
- `airlock witness query --help` shows all filter flags
- Introspection flag precedence: --describe > --schema > --version > subcommand
- `airlock --describe` emits valid JSON matching operator.json structure
- `airlock --schema` emits valid JSON Schema
- `airlock --version` emits "airlock X.Y.Z" format
- `airlock --describe --schema` → operator.json (describe wins)
- `airlock --schema --version` → schema (schema wins)
- No subcommand + no flag → helpful error message, exit 2
- clap validation catches missing required args before dispatch
- operator.json includes all subcommands, exit semantics, refusal codes, witness capabilities
- include_str! embeds operator.json and schema at compile time
- src/commands/ directory exists with stub handlers for all four command groups
- Command beads can create their handler files without touching lib.rs
- Unit tests: parse each subcommand with valid args, verify introspection flag precedence, verify emit function output

title: Integration test suite: CLI tests, determinism, schema validation, witness, refusal coverage
description:
## Goal
Implement the comprehensive integration test suite that validates airlock end-to-end. These tests use the golden fixtures and exercise every command path.

## Deliverables

### tests/cli_tests.rs — CLI integration tests

#### Introspection precedence tests (validates al-jwl)
- test_describe_emits_operator_json: airlock --describe → valid JSON with expected fields
- test_schema_emits_json_schema: airlock --schema → valid JSON Schema
- test_version_emits_semver: airlock --version → "airlock X.Y.Z"
- test_describe_takes_precedence: airlock --describe --schema → operator.json (not schema)
- test_schema_takes_precedence_over_version: airlock --schema --version → schema (not version)

#### Assemble integration tests
- test_assemble_clean_telemetry: assemble with clean_telemetry_only fixtures → matches expected output
- test_assemble_annotated_mode: assemble with annotated_mode fixtures → includes derived text
- test_assemble_telemetry_only_strips_derived: assemble with annotated inputs but TELEMETRY_ONLY mode → derived text stripped
- test_assemble_missing_policy: missing policy file → exit 2 with refusal
- test_assemble_missing_input: missing input file → exit 2 with refusal
- test_assemble_multiple_inputs: multiple --input flags all included

#### Verify integration tests
- test_verify_strict_telemetry: verify with clean_telemetry_only fixtures → STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY, exit 0
- test_verify_annotated: verify with annotated_mode fixtures → RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT, exit 0
- test_verify_boundary_failed: verify with boundary_failed fixtures → BOUNDARY_FAILED, exit 0
- test_verify_require_claim_met: --require-claim RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT on RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT → exit 0
- test_verify_require_claim_not_met: --require-claim STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY on RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT → exit 1
- test_verify_always_emits_manifest: even BOUNDARY_FAILED → manifest file exists

#### Explain integration tests
- test_explain_strict_telemetry: explain clean manifest → shows STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY
- test_explain_annotated: explain annotated manifest → shows blocked reasons
- test_explain_boundary_failed: explain failed manifest → shows findings
- test_explain_missing_manifest: missing file → exit 2 with refusal

#### Witness integration tests
- test_witness_append_on_assemble: assemble → witness record appended with ASSEMBLED
- test_witness_append_on_verify: verify → witness record appended with VERIFIED
- test_witness_no_witness_flag: --no-witness → no record appended
- test_witness_query_filters: populate ledger, query with filters
- test_witness_last: returns most recent
- test_witness_count: returns correct count
- test_witness_append_failure_preserves_exit: read-only ledger → stderr warning, domain exit preserved

### tests/determinism_tests.rs
- test_assemble_deterministic: same inputs 10x → same prompt_payload_hash
- test_manifest_deterministic: same verify inputs 10x → same manifest hash
- test_provenance_deterministic: same inputs 10x → same provenance hash
- test_canonical_json_key_order: construct Value with random key order → canonical output stable

### tests/schema_validation_tests.rs
- test_manifest_validates_against_schema: golden manifest → validates against airlock.v0.schema.json
- test_provenance_validates_against_schema: golden provenance → validates
- test_refusal_validates_against_schema: golden refusal → validates
- test_bad_manifest_fails_schema: malformed manifest → fails validation

### tests/refusal_tests.rs
- Test each RefusalCode produces correct JSON shape
- Test refusal envelope always contains version, outcome, refusal fields
- Test next_command is present when applicable

## Acceptance Criteria
- All tests pass with cargo test
- Tests use golden fixtures from fixtures/ directory
- Tests use assert_cmd for CLI binary invocation
- No flaky tests (determinism is the whole point)
- Witness tests use temp directories (via tempfile crate) for ledger isolation
- Schema validation tests use jsonschema crate
- Coverage: every exit code path (0, 1, 2) tested
- Coverage: every command (assemble, verify, explain, witness query/last/count) tested
- Coverage: every refusal code tested
- assert_cmd and predicates added to dev-dependencies (should already be in Cargo.toml from al-1bo)

title: Prompt assembler: deterministic assembly, field selection, boundary mode filtering
description:
## Goal
Implement the deterministic prompt assembly engine. The assembler takes approved telemetry inputs + policy and produces prompt_payload.json + prompt_provenance.json. Same inputs MUST produce same outputs.

## Deliverables

### src/assembler/mod.rs

#### Core function
```rust
pub fn assemble(
    policy: &AirlockPolicy,
    inputs: &[InputArtifact],
    system_prompt: &str,
    boundary_mode: BoundaryMode,
) -> Result<AssemblyResult, RefusalEnvelope>
```

#### InputArtifact struct
- `path: PathBuf` — source file path (for provenance tracing)
- `content_hash: String` — blake3:{hex} of raw file bytes (pre-computed by caller)
- `data: serde_json::Value` — parsed JSON content (pre-loaded by caller)

Note: The assembler receives pre-loaded InputArtifact structs. File I/O (reading files, computing content hashes) is the command handler's responsibility (al-2bi). The assembler is a pure function over already-loaded data — this keeps it deterministic and testable without touching the filesystem.

#### AssemblyResult struct
- `prompt_payload: serde_json::Value` — the deterministic model-facing content
- `prompt_provenance: PromptProvenance` — proof-carrying source map
- `prompt_payload_hash: String` — blake3 of canonical JSON bytes
- `system_prompt_hash: String` — blake3 of system prompt bytes

#### Assembly logic
1. For each allowed key in policy.allowed_keys:
   - Walk the input artifacts to find matching key paths
   - If found, include the value in the prompt payload
   - Create a ProvenanceRecord for each included field
2. For each derived_text_path in policy:
   - If boundary_mode == TELEMETRY_ONLY: STRIP the field entirely
   - If boundary_mode == ANNOTATED: include it, mark boundary_class as DERIVED_TEXT
3. Build the prompt structure:
   - system message from system_prompt parameter
   - user message from assembled fields
4. Sort all JSON keys canonically
5. Hash the canonical prompt payload bytes

#### Determinism contract
- Same policy + same inputs + same system_prompt + same boundary_mode → identical prompt_payload_hash every time
- Field ordering is canonical (sorted keys)
- No timestamps, random values, or non-deterministic elements in the payload

#### Provenance emission
- One ProvenanceRecord per emitted field/fragment
- Every emitted value has a source_artifact_hash and source_path
- Every emitted value has a policy_rule_id linking it to the allow rule
- DERIVED_TEXT fields are explicitly classified

## Acceptance Criteria
- Determinism test: assemble twice with identical inputs, assert identical prompt_payload_hash
- Boundary mode test: same inputs with ANNOTATED includes derived text, TELEMETRY_ONLY strips it
- Provenance coverage test: every key in prompt_payload has a corresponding ProvenanceRecord
- No prompt bytes without provenance (invariant #1 from prompt provenance section)
- Invalid input JSON returns RefusalEnvelope
- System prompt hash is blake3 of raw system prompt string bytes
- Assembler never touches the filesystem (pure function test — pass in-memory data, no temp files needed)
- Unit tests: clean assembly, assembly with derived text stripping, provenance completeness check

title: CI/CD + release automation: ci.yml, release.yml, five-target matrix, signing, SBOM
description:
## Goal
Implement the CI and release automation matching the mature spine tool posture. Airlock ships the same way as vacuum, hash, lock, pack, fingerprint, canon.

## Deliverables

### .github/workflows/ci.yml
- Trigger: push to main, pull requests
- Steps:
  1. cargo fmt --check
  2. cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
  3. cargo test
  4. cargo build --release
- Rust toolchain: stable, with locked dependencies (--locked)
- Cache: cargo registry + target directory

### .github/workflows/release.yml
- Trigger: push tag matching v*
- Version validation: tag version must match Cargo.toml version
- Five-target build matrix:
  - x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
  - aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
  - x86_64-apple-darwin
  - aarch64-apple-darwin
  - x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
- For each target:
  1. Cross-compile with cross or cargo (native for host targets)
  2. Package archive: airlock-{version}-{target}.tar.gz (Unix) / .zip (Windows)
  3. Archive contains: binary + README.md + LICENSE
- Post-build:
  1. Generate SHA256SUMS from all archives
  2. Keyless signing with cosign (sigstore) for SHA256SUMS
  3. Generate SPDX SBOM artifact
  4. Create GitHub Release with all archives + SHA256SUMS + signature + SBOM
  5. Update Homebrew tap formula at cmdrvl/homebrew-tap (via PR or direct push)

### Homebrew formula template
- Formula: airlock.rb
- Downloads from GitHub Release assets
- SHA256 checksums per platform
- Binary-only install (no build from source)

## Acceptance Criteria
- ci.yml runs fmt, clippy, test, build successfully
- release.yml builds for all 5 targets
- Archives contain exactly: binary + README.md + LICENSE
- SHA256SUMS generated and signed
- SBOM artifact generated
- Version tag must match Cargo.toml (build fails if mismatch)
- Homebrew formula installs correctly
- CI uses --locked for reproducible builds

title: JSON schemas + golden fixtures: policy, manifest, provenance, refusal schemas and test data
description:
## Goal
Create the JSON schema files for validation and golden fixture sets for testing known-good, known-degraded, and known-forbidden boundary cases.

Note: The manifest/output schema (airlock.v0.schema.json) is created by al-jwl and embedded in the binary. This bead creates the remaining validation schemas and all fixtures.

## Deliverables

### schemas/airlock_policy.schema.json
JSON Schema for airlock_policy.yaml (validated after YAML-to-JSON conversion):
- Required: policy_id, version, allowed_keys, forbidden_keys, forbidden_patterns, derived_text_paths, claim_levels
- AllowRule shape: key_path (string, required), description (string, optional), boundary_class (enum, optional)
- ForbiddenPattern shape: pattern (string, required), description (string, optional), artifact_class (string, optional)
- claim_levels: array of enum values (BOUNDARY_FAILED, RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT, STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY)

### schemas/prompt_provenance.schema.json
JSON Schema for prompt_provenance.json:
- Required: version, policy_hash, prompt_payload_hash, boundary_mode, records
- ProvenanceRecord: prompt_path, emitted_value_hash, source_artifact_hash, source_path, transformation_kind, policy_rule_id, boundary_class, admitted_boundary_modes
- Enum constraints on transformation_kind (COPY, NORMALIZE, AGGREGATE, TEMPLATE, DERIVED_ANNOTATION), boundary_class (TELEMETRY, DERIVED_TEXT, FORBIDDEN), boundary_mode (ANNOTATED, TELEMETRY_ONLY)

### schemas/refusal.schema.json
JSON Schema for refusal envelopes:
- Required: version, outcome, refusal
- refusal: code (enum of all E_ codes), message (string), detail (any), next_command (string, optional)

### fixtures/clean_telemetry_only/
Golden fixture for STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY claim:
- policy.yaml — strict policy with no derived text paths allowing cross
- strategy_space.json — input with only structured telemetry (benchmark_summary, unresolved_failure_fields, allowed_toggles, etc.)
- system_prompt.txt — system prompt text
- expected_prompt_payload.json — deterministic expected assembler output
- expected_prompt_provenance.json — expected provenance map (all TELEMETRY class)
- request.json — OpenAI-format wrapped request
- expected_manifest.json — manifest with achieved_claim: STRICT_TELEMETRY_ONLY, boundary_mode: TELEMETRY_ONLY

### fixtures/annotated_mode/
Golden fixture for RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT claim:
- policy.yaml — policy that allows derived text paths (challenge_observation, layout_notes)
- strategy_space.json — input with telemetry AND derived text fields
- system_prompt.txt
- expected_prompt_payload.json — includes derived text
- expected_prompt_provenance.json — mix of TELEMETRY and DERIVED_TEXT classes
- request.json
- expected_manifest.json — achieved_claim: RAW_DOCUMENT_ABSENT, boundary_mode: ANNOTATED, derived_text_field_hits in findings

### fixtures/boundary_failed/
Golden fixture for BOUNDARY_FAILED claim:
- policy.yaml — standard policy
- strategy_space.json — input containing forbidden material (html_path key, SEC archive URL value, raw HTML markup)
- system_prompt.txt
- request.json — request with forbidden content
- expected_manifest.json — achieved_claim: BOUNDARY_FAILED, findings populated with forbidden key/pattern matches

### fixtures/refusal/
Golden fixtures for refusal paths:
- bad_policy.yaml — invalid YAML syntax
- missing_fields_policy.yaml — valid YAML, missing required fields (no policy_id)
- expected_refusal_bad_policy.json — expected RefusalEnvelope with E_BAD_POLICY

## Acceptance Criteria
- All schema files are valid JSON Schema (draft-07 or 2020-12)
- Example policy in fixtures validates against policy schema
- Example provenance in fixtures validates against provenance schema
- Example refusal in fixtures validates against refusal schema
- Example manifest in fixtures validates against schemas/airlock.v0.schema.json (created by al-jwl)
- Each fixture set is internally consistent (expected outputs match what tool should produce given those inputs)
- Fixture strategy_space.json files contain realistic tournament-shaped data with proper field names
- All hashes in expected fixtures use blake3:{hex} format with correct 64-char hex
- Fixture forbidden content includes at least: a forbidden key path, a forbidden regex pattern match (HTML), and a forbidden URL pattern
